findings.title: Full-balance NFT enumeration makes claims unscalable and lets attackers dust wallets into permanent out-of-gas failure

best_agent_match.title: Unbounded NFT enumeration lets attackers dust wallets into permanent claim failure

rejected_candidates.title: Any temporary holder can capture the one-time airdrop because eligibility is based on live balances
rejected_candidates.reason: The contract clearly keys eligibility to the current holder at claim time, and no snapshot or different intended beneficiary is specified in the code. Without an external specification, this is a design choice rather than a code-level vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: The drop becomes first-come-first-served if the contract is underfunded
rejected_candidates.reason: Insufficient ERC20 funding is an external/admin provisioning issue. Failed claims revert before any claim state is burned, so users can retry if the owner later tops up before the deadline.

rejected_candidates.title: Fee-on-transfer or non-standard GRAPES tokens permanently underpay claimants
rejected_candidates.reason: This depends on the configured GRAPES token being fee-on-transfer or otherwise non-standard. The repository does not include such a token or evidence that this deployment uses one, so the issue is too integration-specific and speculative here.

rejected_candidates.title: Owner can permanently pause claims
rejected_candidates.reason: This is an owner-trust/centralization property of the design, not a distinct exploitable vulnerability. The owner already controls when claims start and can sweep leftovers after the period ends.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing event for pauseClaimablePeriod
rejected_candidates.reason: Missing observability does not create realistic protocol-level harm such as loss, theft, insolvency, or denial of service.

rejected_candidates.title: Inefficient iteration over NFT collections
rejected_candidates.reason: The missing early break in the Gamma loop is only a gas inefficiency and is already subsumed by the broader unbounded-enumeration denial-of-service finding.
